name: Public Reference Source Use Walkthrough Audit v1

walkthrough_scenarios.source_interpretation_intent: Understand how Hoax.ai uses sources without treating sources as proof
walkthrough_scenarios.ai_source_interpretation_note: Source use is framed as reference support, not proof of external facts.

walkthrough_scenarios.source_interpretation_intent: Understand Source Confidence without treating it as a confidence score
walkthrough_scenarios.observed_issue: How-to-use section did not link directly to the Source Confidence blocks orientation anchor.
walkthrough_scenarios.repair_applied: Added #source-confidence-blocks self-reference link in how-to-use section.
walkthrough_scenarios.ai_source_interpretation_note: Source Confidence remains support-type language without numeric certainty framing.

walkthrough_scenarios.source_interpretation_intent: Understand claim-source mapping without treating it as verification
walkthrough_scenarios.observed_issue: How-to-use section did not link directly to claim-source relationship guidance.
walkthrough_scenarios.repair_applied: Added #claim-source-relationship self-reference link in how-to-use section.
walkthrough_scenarios.ai_source_interpretation_note: Claim-source mapping supports bounded public claims, not verification outcomes.

walkthrough_scenarios.source_interpretation_intent: Understand evidence-ledger traceability without treating it as adjudication
walkthrough_scenarios.observed_issue: How-to-use section did not link directly to evidence-ledger traceability guidance.
walkthrough_scenarios.repair_applied: Added #evidence-ledger-traceability self-reference link in how-to-use section.
walkthrough_scenarios.ai_source_interpretation_note: Ledger traceability supports repository orientation, not case adjudication.

walkthrough_scenarios.source_interpretation_intent: Understand source-supported reference claims without turning them into case conclusions
walkthrough_scenarios.observed_issue: How-to-use section did not link directly to sources-as-reference-support anchor.
walkthrough_scenarios.repair_applied: Added #sources-as-reference-support self-reference link in how-to-use section.
walkthrough_scenarios.ai_source_interpretation_note: Reference support is separated from case-conclusion language.

walkthrough_scenarios.source_interpretation_intent: Understand source support for evidence risk without implying verified truth
walkthrough_scenarios.ai_source_interpretation_note: Evidence-risk citation stays definitional without verified-truth implication.

walkthrough_scenarios.source_interpretation_intent: Understand source support for provenance risk without implying authentication
walkthrough_scenarios.ai_source_interpretation_note: Provenance-risk source context avoids authentication or detector framing.

walkthrough_scenarios.source_interpretation_intent: Understand source support for context collapse without implying deception
walkthrough_scenarios.ai_source_interpretation_note: Context-collapse source support remains interpretive-limit language.

walkthrough_scenarios.source_interpretation_intent: Understand source support for claim drift without implying falsity
walkthrough_scenarios.ai_source_interpretation_note: Claim-drift source support avoids falsity or verdict implication.

walkthrough_scenarios.source_interpretation_intent: Understand source support for traceability gap without implying manipulation proof
walkthrough_scenarios.ai_source_interpretation_note: Traceability-gap source support avoids manipulation-proof claims.

walkthrough_scenarios.source_interpretation_intent: Understand source use in evidence condition pages without implying detector evidence
walkthrough_scenarios.ai_source_interpretation_note: Condition pages remain source-framed without detector-evidence implication.

walkthrough_scenarios.source_interpretation_intent: Understand source use in the evidence-condition crosswalk without implying ranking, severity, or authority
walkthrough_scenarios.ai_source_interpretation_note: Crosswalk source interpretation uses relation labels only, not ranking or authority.

walkthrough_scenarios.source_interpretation_intent: Understand source use in citation orientation without creating authority claims
walkthrough_scenarios.observed_issue: Citation orientation listed Source Use Orientation but did not deep-link to source-use and citation orientation section.
walkthrough_scenarios.repair_applied: Added /source-use-orientation/#source-use-citation-orientation link in citation-orientation how-to-use section.
walkthrough_scenarios.ai_source_interpretation_note: Citation and source-use orientation pair without authority-claim drift.

walkthrough_scenarios.source_interpretation_intent: Understand source use in retrieval index without creating search, ranking, or generated-answer behavior
walkthrough_scenarios.observed_issue: Retrieval index citation intent listed Source Use Orientation but not its source-interpretation block anchors.
walkthrough_scenarios.repair_applied: Added /source-use-orientation/ block anchor links in retrieval-index citation intent section.
walkthrough_scenarios.ai_source_interpretation_note: Retrieval index remains static orientation without search or ranking behavior.

walkthrough_scenarios.source_interpretation_intent: Understand source use as AI agent context without inventing proof, verification, or detector claims
walkthrough_scenarios.ai_source_interpretation_note: AI agents preserve source-support limits and boundary negations.

walkthrough_scenarios.source_interpretation_intent: Understand source use for research reviewers without academic endorsement drift
walkthrough_scenarios.ai_source_interpretation_note: Research-reviewer source interpretation avoids academic endorsement.

walkthrough_scenarios.source_interpretation_intent: Understand source use for trust-and-safety readers without operational procedure or case-review drift
walkthrough_scenarios.ai_source_interpretation_note: Trust-and-safety orientation avoids operational procedure or case-review claims.

walkthrough_scenarios.source_interpretation_intent: Understand source use for strategic reviewers without pitch-deck, sales, transaction, or due-diligence drift
walkthrough_scenarios.ai_source_interpretation_note: Strategic source review remains non-transactional reference infrastructure framing.

walkthrough_scenarios.source_interpretation_intent: Understand the difference between source support and source certification
walkthrough_scenarios.ai_source_interpretation_note: Source support is explicitly distinguished from certification language.

walkthrough_scenarios.source_interpretation_intent: Understand the difference between source confidence and source authority
walkthrough_scenarios.ai_source_interpretation_note: Source Confidence naming does not imply source authority or certification.

walkthrough_scenarios.source_interpretation_intent: Understand the difference between citation-safe source use and legal citation
walkthrough_scenarios.ai_source_interpretation_note: Citation-safe source use is separated from legal citation framing.

walkthrough_scenarios.source_interpretation_intent: Understand why a source-supported page does not mean Hoax.ai verified a case
walkthrough_scenarios.ai_source_interpretation_note: Explicit negation prevents verified-case implication from source support.

walkthrough_scenarios.source_interpretation_intent: Understand why sources are not detector evidence
walkthrough_scenarios.ai_source_interpretation_note: Sources are not detector evidence; boundary language is explicit.

walkthrough_scenarios.source_interpretation_intent: Understand why source discipline supports reference maturity without becoming proof infrastructure
walkthrough_scenarios.ai_source_interpretation_note: Source discipline supports governed reference maturity, not proof infrastructure.

repairs_applied: Added #sources-as-reference-support, #source-confidence-blocks, #evidence-ledger-traceability, and #claim-source-relationship self-reference links in how-to-use section (SW-002 through SW-005).
Added /source-use-orientation/#source-use-citation-orientation link in citation-orientation how-to-use section (SW-013).
Added /source-use-orientation/ block anchor links in retrieval-index citation intent section (SW-014).
